pairs.hazard: ClickHouse lower() is ASCII-only: 'İ'/'ß'/'É' pass through unchanged; DuckDB lowercases full Unicode. Parity needs ClickHouse lowerUTF8 — and even that diverges (İ->i+U+0307).
ClickHouse upper() is ASCII-only (ß/É unchanged) vs DuckDB full-Unicode (ß->ẞ). upperUTF8 folds but ß->'SS' (length-changing), still != DuckDB ẞ.
lcase is an alias of ASCII-only lower(); 'İ' unchanged vs DuckDB 'i'.
ucase is an alias of ASCII-only upper(); non-ASCII unchanged vs DuckDB folding.
ClickHouse length() counts BYTES (😀->4, 中文->6, ZWJ family->18); DuckDB length() counts code points. Use lengthUTF8/char_length for code-point parity.
Both count code points (😀->1); ClickHouse char_length == lengthUTF8.
Alias of char_length; code-point count in both.
ClickHouse octet_length(varchar)=byte count; DuckDB octet_length has no bare-VARCHAR overload (requires BLOB) and errors.
ClickHouse reverse() reverses BYTES (mangles multi-byte UTF-8 into U+FFFD); DuckDB reverse is grapheme-aware. reverseUTF8 is code-point-aware but still splits combining marks vs DuckDB graphemes.
ClickHouse trim strips ONLY the ASCII space (0x20): NBSP/EM-SPACE left intact; DuckDB additionally strips NBSP and EM SPACE. Neither strips tab/newline.
Same whitespace-set divergence as trim (ASCII space only in ClickHouse).
Same whitespace-set divergence as trim (ASCII space only in ClickHouse).
ClickHouse concat PROPAGATES NULL (any NULL arg -> NULL); DuckDB SKIPS NULLs (concat('a',NULL,'c')='ac', concat(NULL,NULL)='').
ClickHouse concat_ws propagates NULL from any element -> NULL; DuckDB skips NULL elements. NULL separator -> NULL in both.

ClickHouse substring is BYTE-indexed (splits multi-byte chars -> U+FFFD) and start=0 returns '' (not treated as 1); DuckDB is code-point-indexed and clamps start=0 to 1. Negative length also differs. substringUTF8 fixes the multibyte axis only.
See substr: byte indexing + start=0 empty + negative-length semantics differ; substringUTF8 needed for code-point parity.
ClickHouse position(haystack, needle) is 1-based like DuckDB, but argument ORDER and the comma form differ from DuckDB's position(needle IN haystack); DuckDB's comma form is strpos(str,search).
ClickHouse instr is an alias of positionCaseInsensitive — CASE-INSENSITIVE search; DuckDB instr is case-sensitive (instr('Hello','h')=1 in CH vs 0 in DuckDB).
ClickHouse replace == replaceAll: replaces ALL occurrences, same as DuckDB replace.

DuckDB regexp_replace(s,p,r) replaces only the FIRST match (no 'g' flag); the generator now emits replaceRegexpOne (first-only), result-identical for the common case. RESIDUAL divergence: empty-pattern behavior differs (ClickHouse no-op vs DuckDB inserts at every position) and RE2-vs-RE2 flag handling — hence conditionally-equivalent, not identical. The 'g'/Trino all-form maps to replaceRegexpAll.
pairs.provenance: REPORT-clickhouse-differential-probe-2026-07-13.md#replace-regexp-first-vs-all | generator mapping fixed 2026-07-13 (BUGS-clickhouse-generator-mappings); re-verify vs live FE

pairs.hazard: Group semantics aligned: group 0 = whole match, group N = Nth capture; probed backslash-free to avoid the literal-escaping confound.
ClickHouse match() is a partial (unanchored) RE2 search returning UInt8 0/1; DuckDB regexp_matches returns BOOLEAN. Same match set for equivalent patterns; return type differs (0/1 vs true/false).
ClickHouse leftPad pads by BYTES; DuckDB lpad pads by code points. leftPadUTF8 is the code-point variant. ASCII aligned.
ClickHouse rightPad pads by BYTES; DuckDB rpad by code points. rightPadUTF8 for parity. ASCII aligned.
ClickHouse ascii returns the first BYTE (emoji->240); DuckDB returns the first code point (emoji->128512). ASCII inputs aligned.
Aligned incl. negative n (all-but-last-|n|). Probe-verified on ASCII.
Aligned on ASCII probes.
repeat(s,0)='' and negative count -> '' in both.
Code-point-wise substitution; ASCII probe aligned.

ClickHouse format uses positional {0},{1} placeholders (Python/.NET style); DuckDB format uses fmt/printf %-style. Incompatible spec dialects — never map by name. (ClickHouse printf() matches DuckDB printf.)
Both use C printf %-specifiers; probe 'x=5' aligned.
ClickHouse soundex('Robert')='R163' matches DuckDB (which needs no extension here); rename only.
Standard alphabet; 'abc'->'YWJj' in both.
'YWJj'->'abc' in both.

Same digest but return SHAPE differs: ClickHouse MD5 returns FixedString(16) raw bytes; DuckDB md5 returns a lowercase hex VARCHAR. Wrap ClickHouse in lower(hex(...)) to compare. NULL-propagates in both.
Same digest; ClickHouse SHA1 returns raw bytes (FixedString), DuckDB sha1 returns hex VARCHAR — hex() wrap required.
Same digest; ClickHouse returns raw bytes vs DuckDB hex VARCHAR — hex() wrap required.
abs(-2147483648) (INT32 min): ClickHouse widens the result and returns 2147483648; DuckDB THROWS an overflow error.
Float division by zero: ClickHouse returns NULL; DuckDB returns +inf.
Plain multiplication aligned; overflow follows the promotion/wrap axis noted for integer ops.
modulo by zero: ClickHouse THROWS (Division by zero, code 153); DuckDB returns NULL. Non-zero modulo (incl. negative operands) aligned.
ClickHouse round() uses banker's rounding (round-half-to-EVEN): round(2.5)=2, round(-2.5)=-2; DuckDB rounds half-AWAY-from-zero: 3 and -3. roundBankers matches ClickHouse round.
ClickHouse roundBankers == its default round (half-even); equals DuckDB only when DuckDB round is forced to banker's, which it is not by default.
Truncation toward zero aligned incl. negatives.
Aligned incl. negatives (floor(-2.5)=-3).
Aligned incl. negatives (ceil(-2.5)=-2).
Alias of ceil; aligned.
sqrt(-1): ClickHouse returns NULL (nan absorbed to NULL); DuckDB THROWS 'cannot take square root of a negative'.
NAME COLLISION + domain: ClickHouse log(x) is NATURAL log (== ln), so mapping DuckDB ln->log is value-correct, but ln(0)/ln(-1): ClickHouse returns NULL, DuckDB THROWS.
DuckDB single-arg log(x) is base-10; the generator now emits log10(x), result-identical (never ClickHouse log, which is natural log).
pairs.provenance: REPORT-clickhouse-differential-probe-2026-07-13.md#log-collision | generator mapping fixed 2026-07-13 (BUGS-clickhouse-generator-mappings); re-verify vs live FE

pairs.hazard: Base-10 log; log10(1000)=3 in both (map DuckDB log10 to ClickHouse log10, never to log).
log2(8)=3 in both.
factorial(-1): ClickHouse returns 1; DuckDB THROWS.
pow(-8,0.5) (complex result): ClickHouse returns NULL; DuckDB returns nan. Finite real cases aligned.
See pow: pow(-8,0.5) NULL vs nan; finite cases aligned.
sign of negative/zero aligned; float NaN not re-probed here.
Same predicate; ClickHouse returns UInt8 0/1, DuckDB BOOLEAN true/false.
Same predicate; 0/1 vs true/false return type.
gcd(0,5): ClickHouse THROWS (Division by zero); DuckDB returns 5. Non-zero pairs aligned.
lcm(4,6)=12 aligned for non-zero args.
ClickHouse bin(5)='00000101' (zero-padded to a full byte); DuckDB bin(5)='101' (no leading zeros).
hex(255)='FF', hex('A')='41' in both.
Rename/behaviour aligned on hex decode; ClickHouse returns String, DuckDB BLOB (type-layer).
DuckDB xor is bitwise; the generator now emits bitXor(a,b), result-identical (not ClickHouse logical xor, and not the invalid `^` operator).
pairs.provenance: REPORT-clickhouse-differential-probe-2026-07-13.md#xor-logical-vs-bitwise | generator mapping fixed 2026-07-13 (BUGS-clickhouse-generator-mappings); re-verify vs live FE

pairs.hazard: exp(1)/exp(2) agree to full double precision.
acos(2) (out of domain): ClickHouse NULL; DuckDB THROWS. In-domain values aligned.
asin(2) out of domain: ClickHouse NULL; DuckDB THROWS. In-domain aligned.
In-domain aligned to ~double precision; out-of-domain follows the NULL-vs-throw contract (acosh(0)).
In-domain aligned; |x|>=1 follows NULL-vs-throw contract.
Aligned to double precision.
Aligned to double precision.
Aligned to double precision.
Aligned to double precision.
Aligned to double precision.
Aligned to double precision.
Aligned to double precision.
Aligned to double precision.
Aligned to double precision.
cbrt(27)=3 aligned.
Aligned to double precision.
Aligned to double precision.
lgamma(5)=3.178054 aligned.
Value-correct for 1970+, but ClickHouse Date/DateTime cannot represent pre-1970: toDate('1969-12-31') clamps and year() returns 1970. DuckDB handles the full proleptic range.
Aligned for representable (>=1970) dates.
Aligned for representable dates.
Aligned for representable dates.
Aligned when session timezones match; ClickHouse DateTime is timezone-typed (DateTime('tz') / session_timezone) so an unpinned zone silently shifts results.
Aligned.
Aligned.

DuckDB/Trino millisecond(t) = seconds-within-minute*1000 + ms; the generator now emits (toSecond(t)*1000 + toMillisecond(t)), result-identical (ClickHouse toMillisecond alone is the sub-second component only). Verified: 30123/0/5789/56001 all match.
pairs.provenance: REPORT-clickhouse-differential-probe-2026-07-13.md#datetime-millisecond | generator mapping fixed 2026-07-13 (BUGS row 6), live-differential-verified vs ClickHouse 26.5.1.1 + DuckDB 1.5.4

pairs.hazard: ClickHouse toDayOfWeek is ISO Mon=1..Sun=7; DuckDB dayofweek is Sun=0..Sat=6. Sunday: 7 vs 0.
Leap-day pinned: 2024-02-29 -> 60 in both.
Aligned.

ClickHouse toWeek default mode 0 is Sunday-based (2023-01-01 -> 1); DuckDB week is ISO-8601 (-> 52). The generator renders DuckDB week faithfully as WEEK/toWeek (NOT toISOWeek) because the same rewrite would corrupt native ClickHouse week on ClickHouse->ClickHouse (pipe desugaring) — the correct source-aware fix is deferred (see SPIKE-source-aware-generator-transforms). Map to toISOWeek for ISO parity when transpiling.
ClickHouse toYearWeek default mode 0 (Sunday weeks, calendar year): 2024-12-30 -> 202452; DuckDB yearweek is ISO (-> 202501). Numbering AND year differ at boundaries.

Aligned on the intersecting unit set for representable dates, but return type differs for some units (ClickHouse dateTrunc('month',DateTime) yielded a Date-shaped value vs DuckDB TIMESTAMP) and the 1970 floor applies.
Alias of date_trunc; same caveats.
With an explicit unit and matched session zones the day-diff aligned (60); ClickHouse requires the unit as first arg and is timezone-typed. 1970 floor applies to operands.
See date_diff; ClickHouse datediff needs a unit arg.
Value-correct for 1970+ (2024-02 -> 29), but 1900-02-15 -> ClickHouse '1970-01-31' (Date range floor) vs DuckDB '1900-02-28'.
Same output ('March') but ClickHouse name is the case-SENSITIVE camelCase monthName; the lowercase monthname is NOT a case-insensitive alias and will not resolve.
Both bind once per query; ClickHouse now() returns DateTime (second precision, timezone-typed) vs DuckDB TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE (microsecond). Precision/type differ.
ClickHouse current_date/today() is session-zone dependent (Date type, 1970 floor); do not push where a specific zone/range must hold.
NAME COLLISION: DuckDB to_days(n) constructs an INTERVAL of n days; ClickHouse has no to_days — the day-number is toRelativeDayNum(date). Completely different intent.
Different signature: ClickHouse age('unit', start, end) returns an integer count in that unit; DuckDB age(ts, ts) returns an INTERVAL. Never map by name.
ClickHouse timezone() returns the server/session timezone name (String); DuckDB has no zero-arg timezone() scalar (it is a shift function elsewhere) — session-only, not pushable.
range(5)->[0..4], range(1,5)->[1..4] in both (list rendering aside).
One level of nesting flattened in both.
Join aligned incl. NULL elements skipped by both ('a',NULL,'c' -> 'a,c').

NAME/TYPE collision: DuckDB cardinality operates ONLY on MAP (errors on a list); ClickHouse cardinality is an alias of length (array element count). Map DuckDB list length to ClickHouse length, not via cardinality.
Both collect into an array; ordering is undefined without ORDER BY and NULL-handling of groupArray differs (groupArray drops NULLs; use groupArrayIf/arrays for NULL retention).
Empty group: ClickHouse sum() -> 0; DuckDB -> NULL. Also ClickHouse sum silently OVERFLOW-WRAPS a fixed-width Int64 (max+1 -> Int64 min) where DuckDB widens. All-NULL input -> NULL in both.
Empty group: ClickHouse avg() -> nan; DuckDB -> NULL. All-NULL -> NULL in both; populated groups aligned.
count(expr) skips NULLs; count()/count(*) over empty -> 0 in both.
Empty group: ClickHouse min() -> type default (0); DuckDB -> NULL. All-NULL -> NULL in both.
Empty group: ClickHouse max() -> type default (0); DuckDB -> NULL. All-NULL -> NULL in both.
stddevPop({1,2,3})=0.8165 aligned.
Sample stddev aligned.
Bare stddev = sample in both (=1 for {1,2,3}).
varPop({1,2,3})=0.6667 aligned.
Sample variance aligned.
Pearson correlation aligned (0.981981).
Population covariance aligned.
Sample covariance aligned.
median({1,2,3})=2 aligned, but ClickHouse median is an alias for the approximate quantile(0.5) (reservoir) — exact only for small/simple inputs; DuckDB median is exact.

Value aligned on the probe, but ClickHouse quantile uses parametric syntax quantile(level)(x) and is APPROXIMATE; DuckDB quantile_cont/quantile is exact and takes level as an argument. Different call shape + exactness.
ClickHouse any(x) == any_value; order-dependent / nondeterministic without ORDER BY in both.
argMax(y,x) returns y at the max x; aligned with DuckDB arg_max. Ties nondeterministic in both.
argMin(y,x) aligned with DuckDB arg_min.
Both bucket values, but ClickHouse histogram(bins)(x) is an approximate adaptive-bin aggregate with a different signature and result shape than DuckDB histogram (exact value->count map).
DuckDB string_agg(x,sep) has no single-function ClickHouse equal; the idiom is arrayStringConcat(groupArray(x),sep). Ordering undefined without ORDER BY.
Same as string_agg: no direct ClickHouse group_concat; compose groupArray + arrayStringConcat. Ordering undefined.
Both compute Shannon entropy of value frequencies; base and exact/approx handling not fully re-probed — verify before pushing.
1,2,3 over ORDER BY in both.
Standard rank aligned.
Dense rank aligned.
ntile(2) bucketing aligned.
first_value over running frame aligned.
last_value over default running frame aligned in both.
0,0.5,1 in both.
Out-of-partition default: ClickHouse lag (lagInFrame) fills the type DEFAULT (0) for the missing first row; DuckDB lag returns NULL.
Out-of-partition default: ClickHouse lead fills type default (0) past the last row; DuckDB returns NULL.
Before the Nth row exists ClickHouse returns the type DEFAULT (0); DuckDB returns NULL.
Non-deterministic; never pushable/comparable. ClickHouse rand() returns UInt32 range; DuckDB random() returns [0,1) double — even the range differs.
Session/zone-dependent current date; Date type (1970 floor) in ClickHouse. Not pushable to a fixed value.
Both return an engine version string but the values are engine-specific and never comparable/pushable.
Session identity string; ClickHouse currentUser()/current_user, values environment-specific, not pushable.
Session catalog name; environment-specific, not pushable.
Non-deterministic UUID; same distribution, never comparable/pushable.
Constructs a MAP in both but the CONSTRUCTOR SHAPE differs: ClickHouse map(k1,v1,k2,v2,...) (flat kv list) vs DuckDB map([keys],[values]) (two arrays). Value-equal, call-incompatible.
First non-NULL agrees.
